events_calendar: Time Out Houston Things to Do

restaurant_openings.name: Alturas Mexican Cafe
restaurant_openings.summary: A casual, family-owned restaurant in the Heights serving traditional Mexican dishes based on family recipes.

restaurant_openings.name: Hypsi
restaurant_openings.summary: Located in the luxury Heights Hotel Daphne, Hypsi hosts both travelers and locals with a menu from Chef Terrence.

restaurant_openings.name: Long Weekend
restaurant_openings.summary: Fans of the TV series Yellowstone will feel right at home at this Western-inspired spot in the Lazybrook/Timbergrove neighborhood.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 2044 East T C Jester Boulevard, Houston, Texas 77008

restaurant_openings.name: Star Rover
restaurant_openings.summary: A throwback steakhouse offering approachable dinners where diners choose their cut of beef and choice of sides.

restaurant_openings.name: The Green Room
restaurant_openings.summary: A 26-seat restaurant in the Heights featuring dishes from James Beard Award-winning chef Tom Cunanan and Daniel Davidson.

restaurant_openings.name: Exilio Latin Flair
restaurant_openings.summary: The latest addition to the Harlow District transports diners to Spain and Latin America through a diverse mix of flavors.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 2817 West Dallas Street, BLDG C, Houston, Texas 77019

restaurant_openings.name: Zaranda
restaurant_openings.summary: Created by James Beard Award-winner Hugo Ortega, Zaranda offers traditional Mexican flavors in a refined setting.

restaurant_openings.name: Jantra
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef Benchawon Jabthong Painter has expanded her culinary reach with this new Thai concept.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 3401 Harrisburg Boulevard, STE g-1, Houston, Texas 77003

restaurant_openings.summary: This newest nightlife destination from the team behind Melrose was developed as a Mexico City-style lounge.

restaurant_openings.name: Pinkerton’s Barbecue
restaurant_openings.summary: The second Houston location of the acclaimed barbecue spot takes over the former 59 Diner space.

restaurant_openings.name: Crawfish Cafe and Pho Prime
restaurant_openings.summary: This two-for-one concept from Mamba Hospitality Group brings together classic Vietnamese and Cajun flavors.

restaurant_openings.name: Captain Mc’s Seafood
restaurant_openings.summary: Local fisherman Captain Fred delivers his catch of the day from his boat directly to this Third Ward restaurant's kitchen. The recipes were developed with help from local chefs, featuring fried crab fingers, battered black drum, and Gulf shrimp po’boys.

restaurant_openings.name: Jantra
restaurant_openings.summary: From the team behind Street To Kitchen, this new tasting counter offers just one seating a night. The $175 multi-course menu moves beyond Thai food to feature global ingredients like Japanese A5 wagyu and Texas okra.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 3401 HARRISBURG BLVD SUITE G-1, HOUSTON, TX 77003

restaurant_openings.name: La Rosa
restaurant_openings.summary: This Florence-style sandwich shop in the Tlaquepaque Market offers a limited menu of three sandwiches, including mortadella with pistachio pesto. The sandwiches are known to sell out quickly after doors open at noon.
1111 is an upscale cocktail lounge and restaurant inspired by Mexico City. The food menu features Gulf prawn empanadas and whole charcoal-grilled seabass, while the bar serves masa old fashioneds.

restaurant_openings.name: Kirkwood
restaurant_openings.summary: Located in the Energy Corridor, this restaurant serves American classics like steaks and seafood. Highlights include caviar service and tableside carved chateaubriand, set under a unique Astrodome-inspired ceiling installation.

restaurant_openings.name: Killer Burger
restaurant_openings.summary: This Portland-based burger spot has opened its first Houston location in the Arts District. It is famous for the Peanut Butter Pickle Bacon Burger and offers various house-made sauces like habanero bomb sauce.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 1909 TAYLOR ST SUITE A, HOUSTON, TX 77007

restaurant_openings.name: Anthony's New York Italian
restaurant_openings.summary: An offshoot of the Russo’s pizza chain, this River Oaks spot is a red sauce Italian steakhouse. It combines traditional Italian favorites with a steakhouse atmosphere.

restaurant_openings.name: Atlantic Ocean
restaurant_openings.summary: A coastal restaurant on Washington Ave from an Atlanta-based chef. The menu is seafood-heavy with Southern influences, taking over the former Passerella space.

restaurant_openings.name: Exilio Latin Flair
restaurant_openings.summary: Located in Montrose’s Harlow District, Exilio offers a menu with Latin and Spanish influences. It joins the neighborhood as a new destination for diverse regional flavors.

restaurant_openings.name: Osteria Di Mercato
restaurant_openings.summary: This 30-seat Italian restaurant in West University is a dinner-only extension of the Mercato and Company market. It offers a cozy atmosphere for Italian dining and wine.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 3642 UNIVERSITY BLVD, WEST UNIVERSITY PLACE, TEXAS 77005

restaurant_openings.name: Truffle & Rye
restaurant_openings.summary: A new cafe and market in Richmond featuring sourdough banana bread and ricotta-fig toast. It offers a local spot for fresh baked goods and specialized market items.

restaurant_openings.name: Birdies Icehouse
restaurant_openings.summary: This casual icehouse in the East End features an indoor-outdoor setup at East River 9. It provides a relaxed environment for drinks and bar food with views of the Houston skyline.

restaurant_openings.name: The Green Room
restaurant_openings.summary: A 26-seat enclosed porch in The Heights offering a quieter, more focused dining experience. The menu changes frequently, focusing on seasonal European-inspired dishes.

restaurant_openings.name: Toga
restaurant_openings.summary: A new izakaya in the River Oaks District specializing in yakitori. The restaurant focuses on binchotan-grilled skewers and traditional Japanese small plates.

restaurant_openings.name: Yuma
restaurant_openings.summary: Starting as a popular pop-up, Yuma has moved into a permanent space on Washington Ave. The menu specializes in Cuban and Brazilian sandwiches made on fresh-baked bread.

restaurant_openings.name: Aga's
restaurant_openings.summary: Aga's, the Indian-Pakistani restaurant that ranked No. 1 on the Houston Chronicle’s Top 100 restaurants list, will open its second location in Katy on June 24. This marks the halal restaurant's first new restaurant since it opened in 2001.
restaurant_openings.venue_or_address: 102 W. Grand Parkway S., Suite 100, Katy

restaurant_openings.name: Yuma
restaurant_openings.summary: Yuma opens Friday on Washington Avenue, bringing Houston Cuban and Brazilian sandwiches from its popup, plus a Latin-forward bar. The restaurant is located in the former Ninja Ramen space.

restaurant_openings.name: Jantra
restaurant_openings.summary: Jantra is the latest project from chef Benchawan Jabthong Painter and husband Graham Painter, the team behind Street to Kitchen. It is an 8-seat tasting counter concept connected to Street to Kitchen in the East End.

restaurant_openings.name: Oru
restaurant_openings.summary: A design-forward Japanese restaurant in the Heights neighborhood from the team behind Neo and Kira, where minimalism and whimsy meet.

restaurant_openings.name: Zaranda
restaurant_openings.summary: Hugo Ortega’s new Zaranda in downtown Houston spotlights grilled seafood and a showstopping tableside Caesar salad.

restaurant_openings.name: Hypsi
restaurant_openings.summary: Located inside the stylish Hotel Daphne, chef Terrence Gallivan offers an Italian menu with imaginative dishes.

restaurant_openings.name: Annam
restaurant_openings.summary: Annam, a restaurant from the MF Sushi team, explores fine-dining Vietnamese food in Houston’s Autry Park.

restaurant_openings.name: Late August
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef-owner Chris Williams makes the Ion, once the home of a Sears, a restaurant destination with an Afro-Mexican menu.

restaurant_openings.name: Latuli
restaurant_openings.summary: Bryan Caswell returns to the Houston dining scene with Latuli in Memorial, serving seafood, pizza and other hits from his past.

restaurant_openings.name: Jane & the Lion Bakehouse
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef Jane Wild’s bakery reflects a devotion to community and craft, offering everything from savory hand pies to dramatic cream-topped pies.

restaurant_openings.name: Kandahari
restaurant_openings.summary: An Afghan restaurant in Houston's Hillcroft area where Afghan cooking shines through flavorful kabobs, pulao and tandoori bread.

restaurant_openings.name: Grace's Cafe
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef-owner Grace Grant cooks up slow-braised oxtails, smoky jerk chicken and other Jamaican classics in Missouri City.

restaurant_openings.name: Yiayia's Greek Kitchen
restaurant_openings.summary: A Pappas family restaurant in Houston that serves Greek classics with upscale flair, earning a rare four-star review.

restaurant_openings.name: Charm Taphouse & BBQ
restaurant_openings.summary: A Conroe restaurant where smoky meats and Thai curries share the menu with surprising ease.

restaurant_openings.name: Burmalicious
restaurant_openings.summary: Suu Khin, a former 'MasterChef' finalist, shares Burmese culture through her Burmese popup restaurant.

events_calendar.name: Spamilton Roasts Hamilton in a Funny But Loving Way at Stages
events_calendar.summary: Spamilton: An American Parody, tells the story of how Lin-Manuel Miranda decides to save Broadway theater by coming up with a new form of musical theater. The spoof by Tony Award-winner Gerard Alessandrini (Forbidden Broadway), uses the characters of Hamilton to take apart any number of Broadway’s treasured tropes and musical stylings. The production, now […] The post Spamilton Roasts Hamilton in a Funny But Loving Way at Stages appeared first on Houston Press .

events_calendar.name: A$AP Rocky Brings His Don’t Be Dumb Tour to Houston
events_calendar.summary: Running through almost 40 songs, Harlem-born rapper A$AP Rocky brought his Don’t Be Dumb tour to Houston Saturday night in a two-hour, action-packed tour de force. He filled the Toyota Center with flying helicopter props and used a megaphone for a microphone. Don’t Be Dumb marks A$AP’s return to the center stage of hip hop […] The post A$AP Rocky Brings His Don’t Be Dumb Tour to Houston appeared first on Houston Press .

events_calendar.name: The Hunchback of Notre Dame is Musical Theater at its Best
events_calendar.summary: Wherever Houston Broadway Theatre appears, it should erect a lighting rod on the roof because its fiery productions set the space ablaze. The Hobby’s Zilkha Hall is still smoldering after HBT’s searing premiere Next to Normal (2024). Its cult hit American Psycho (2025) almost burned the place down. Now the Wortham’s Cullen Theater should boost […] The post The Hunchback of Notre Dame is Musical Theater at its Best appeared first on Houston Press .

events_calendar.name: Greezy Wheels Keep Turning
events_calendar.summary: The famous Armadillo World Headquarters made Austin’s music scene a beacon for musicians and fans. In just a ten-year span from 1970 to 1980, the venue helped create the cosmic country movement and cemented Austin’s place in music history. No one embodies this spirit more than Greezy Wheels. The band famously set the stage for Willie Nelson to have his first performance at […] The post Greezy Wheels Keep Turning appeared first on Houston Press .

events_calendar.name: Houston Concert Watch 6/17: Todd Rundgren, Khalid and More
events_calendar.summary: Todd Rundgren can’t win. He can play his hits and please the more casual fans or he can play deep cuts, satisfying the “Hot Toddies.” The post Houston Concert Watch 6/17: Todd Rundgren, Khalid and More appeared first on Houston Press .

See the latest performing arts productions and theater happenings opening across Houston from Monday, June 15 to Sunday, June 21, 2026. events_calendar.summary: The post Hone your photo skills & save big at Precision Camera’s Summer Expo in The Woodlands appeared first on 365 Things to Do in Houston . events_calendar.name: Cabo Verde vs. Saudi Arabia (FIFA World Cup)
events_calendar.summary: This Group H match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup features Cabo Verde against Saudi Arabia. The game will be played at 7pm at Houston Stadium.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Overnight Camping at Space Center Houston
events_calendar.summary: An opportunity to sleep in a tent under the Falcon 9 rocket at NASA's official visitor center. This immersive experience is a highlight of the June calendar.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Meow Wolf Houston: Radio Tave
events_calendar.summary: An immersive art experience that transports visitors into a multidimensional radio station. It features work from over 100 artists in a colorful kaleidoscope.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Artechouse Houston Exhibit
events_calendar.summary: A 26,000-square-foot digital art space that combines technology and creativity. The venue includes several viewing rooms and a dedicated cocktail bar.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Art Club at POST Houston
events_calendar.summary: A hybrid museum and nightclub located in a transformed mail processing center. The space features large-scale installations and immersive cultural experiences.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Miller Outdoor Theatre Performances
events_calendar.summary: Free outdoor performances including theater, dance, and music in the heart of Hermann Park. It remains one of the city's most cherished cultural institutions.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Candlelight Concerts
events_calendar.summary: Musical performances held in intimate settings across the city, illuminated by thousands of candles. These events offer unique tributes to popular artists.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do

events_calendar.name: Houston Museum Free Days
events_calendar.summary: Several major institutions offer free admission on specific days, including the Museum of Fine Arts and the Museum of Natural Science.

source.name: Time Out Houston Things to Do
